name: golang-github-google-uuid-dev
title: golang-github-google-uuid-dev
summary: generates and inspects UUIDs based on RFC 4122
description:
generates and inspects UUIDs based on RFC 4122
This package generates and inspects UUIDs based on RFC 4122
(http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4122) and DCE 1.1: Authentication and
Security Services.
.
This package is based on the "github.com/pborman/uuid" package. It differs
from these earlier packages in that a UUID is a 16 byte array rather than
a byte slice. One loss due to this change is the ability to represent an
invalid UUID (vs a NIL UUID).
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-google-uuid-dev because generates and inspects UUIDs based on RFC 4122.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-google-uuid-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-google-uuid-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-google-uuid-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
